failed_cases.message_text: Need Advice on how to tackle the hotel management Hi guys, posting this from my alt as this is a bit embarrassing. So I'm staying in a 3 star hotel in NCR and it is company provided accomodation. I've been staying here for about 10 days now. There's a kettle in the room and I bought milk thinking I would drink it before sleep as I recently joined gym. I set it up to heat and went to washroom to wash my face and in that short span the milk boils and gets out of the kettle and into the socket below. There's a loud sound and then the AC stops (everything else is working). I think the fuse got blown and the AC was connected to it, as it is just above where the kettle is placed. Now, I need advice on how what my next steps should be? Do I tell the hotel staff? I'll only be staying here till 24th March. Will they penalize me for this? I don't know how to reach out as this is my first time staying in a hotel and my first company as well. Please help. TLDR: Shorted hotel room fuse because of kettle and AC is not working now. Need advice on next steps.

failed_cases.message_text: Based on my experience, I would recommend considering a Bluestar non-inverter AC in 2025, if still available. I've found that while inverter ACs may struggle to cool effectively after a few years, non-inverter models, despite inconsistent temperature regulation, provide significant cooling. Their simpler, refrigerator-like technology makes maintenance affordable. Although non-inverter ACs are often considered less energy-efficient, their intense cooling often leads to users switching them off during the night, potentially offsetting higher power consumption. My seven-year-old Daikin 1.5-ton inverter AC has been problematic, while an eight-year-old 1.0-ton Bluestar non-inverter unit performs very well. Even the Daikin service technician suggested purchasing a non-inverter model. Apparently, even affluent consumers are now seeking out these non-inverter units.

failed_cases.message_text: Hello viewers. Today I am going to share my view on LGWFT7519PR automatic Top Load Washing machine. It is one of the best washing machine that I have operated till now. I have purchased this washing machine around 10 years back and till date I have not faced any issues. The Engineer who came for the delivery and installation was great and I still remember he gave some of the tips for the maintenance. Now some of the Great features: > This Washing machine uses very less Electricity. > Less amount or only the required amount of water is used and hence there wont be any of the wastage. > No need to keep checking for the options as this is Fully automatic. > The fuzzy option which automatically sets the best washing mode calculating as per the load makes our work even easier. Just dump all the clothes that needs to be washed and cleaned and then Hit the Fuzzy key. All set the machine will work on its own. > Very less noise in comparison to the other machines. > This machine style and design is very simple but features are really amazing. It has got 8 other programs in addition like quick wash, gentle wash, Tub clean, strong wash which is for jeans or strong clothes. Personally I feel this product is very useful and if we look for the service centers we can find many of the authorized service centers. Only thing we need to do is to call at the customer care number and then raise the problem. LG has a good network and they are providing good support as well. I will rate this washing 4/5 in comparison to its features and also Lg's support. A definite recommended product for a Family.

failed_cases.message_text: Folks at LG seem to be having a problem, in replacing a ''broken Spin tub lid'' since more than two months. I purchased a LG WP-9033 from a local electronics dealer in Bangalore, after exchanging it to my old workhorse of 8 years, Videocon V-NA-400T. I rue the decision to this day. At the time of selling the machine, I wasnt informed that the said product would be ''discontinued'' in three months. I am told now, when I have logged a complaint(M-52176) that the product has been discontinued, and is a challenge for the folks at LG to find a replacement for the broken component. This inspite of still being in the warranty period. As I have been using other LG products(TV, Refrigerator) from LG, I was biased into buying the same brand. I was in for a great shock. The lint filter broke in the first one hour of the machine being delivered at home. The so called service technician said I had to pay, since they were not responsible for the damage. After a high drama, he relented and replaced the broken lint, free of cost after two days. The same pathetic service is continuing with LG(Not sure if this is the case in other cities, but in Bangalore(Ph: 26627788 - Contact person Manohar/Sanjeev), dont ever try calling the above number. Not speaking for others, but I for one will never ever buy another LG product for my home, no matter how good the product is. BTW, I am planning in replacing my LG washing machine sometime this dussera/diwali season to maybe : samsung, Whirlpool etc. Are they any good!!

failed_cases.message_text: Last weekend I booked a LG F1256NDP5 6KG Washing Machine from Videks, Adyar, Chennai Cost of Washing machine: Rs.28700 We already have an IFB Digital 7KG @my home town. We are happy with the machine. Its running fine for the past 3 years. Except for one service we had to do because of the drum rotation got stuck. We didn't know why. As it was in the warranty perioed it was fixed free of cost and only the transportation cost we had to bear. Because services cannot be done onsite for digital models(told by IFB customer service). After a long wait and lots of reading on reviews, I decided to stick to the new technology in the market. Yes its only the Inverter Direct Drive that attracted me to buy LG. I booked it on Sat and I got it delivered on Sunday. LG representative came on Monday for installation. He prepared and setup the machine for use. Couple of accessories he brought. : ) Stand with wheels and Washing Machine cover - Rs. 1500 (No bills) didnt want to dissappoint him so paid it of. So in 30Min time he set it up for use and explained the basis and how to use it. I tried it out right away with all my piled up cloths. I used the cotton 60 1200 w/o prewash. It took 2:30 Min for the complete wash cycle. Once you switch on the machine it give a nice tune during startup. Even beep's when changing options. Once the wash cycle is complete it gives an end tune as well. I really liked it. : ) Intrestingly this machine doesnt flash out the time immediatly when we select the options. After setting up all the options and press the start button It rotates for 15sec in both directions and flashes the max. time for the wash cycle. I suppose it detects the load on the machine. I was impressed on this. I felt the depth of the drum is really small and that makes it very compact. Doesnt take much space just 2.5 feet by 2 feet. So the depth of the drum should just be around 18inches or less than that. After pressing start, keenly observed it for a while. It started taking the water in. Even when taking the water in it doesnt do it in one shot. It takes in some water and rotates in one direction and takes some more and rotates in the other direction. Once enough water is taken inand heated up the wash cycle continued for 1 hour and 30min. During all this it was really silent and there was hardly any vibration or jerk as I observed. I could feel very mild vibration only when I touch it, as on a cd player : ) a little more than that. But when it was doing the spin action @1200 there was quite some vibration. The overall wash quality was impressive. I could see even the dirt on the shirt collars were removed. And this could be achieved on any front loading one and the trick is when loading cloths you have to load it as a 'U' stack. Dont just roll it and throw it in. Stack all the cloths by straighting them and load it along the drum in a 'U' shape. this way I was able to load efficiently and this helps the wash efficiency as well. This was suggested to me by the LG representative. Collectively this machine scores good. I would give a score of 8/10. Even I saw the electricity usage very very less. This had an A+ rating because of the Inverter direct drive, I suppose. It has just been a couple of weeks of use. I hope it does well for a long time :) My weekends are more pleasent nowadays !

failed_cases.message_text: Hello friends today I m going to share my experience of LG washing machine. My mother was suffering from some physical problem. She was unable to work hard. But she has to wash his clothes beacuse of our daily needs she has to wash her clothes by hand. This was very painful for her. Mostly I used to wash her clothes when she was unable to sit properly on the floor. Home maid is not available in my colony. But when I was busy in doing something she used to wash and this caused her became more ill. Sometimes after coming back from his office my father had to wash his clothes. This was very hurting and painful for me. I was very concerned about My mother's health and I thought we must need a washing machine for our entire family. So we decided to bought LG washing machine. we all were very happy that our all problems has shorted out. One day I reached home and I was astonished to see my mom washing clothes by hands. Inspite of having washing machine in our home I asked her the reason for washing clothes by hands. then she replied that "Machine is still working but never worked good for heavy dirty clothes. it never clean clothing spot stain. It is also useless for cleaning collar and cuff of shirts. This machine does not clean white clothes. so I rub by hands it's collar and spot that's why I wash clothes by hands otherwise u can not wear this clothes. After wash I drain in machine. it's drain part is olso not very good. washing part requires a large quantity of water. it is only ok for drain in rainy season." After this incident I realised that our problem is still remain. And then I changed this useless washing machine.

Automatic washing + drying with a single press of button is your single most important criteria, then this machine is for you. I loadit, set the program, select drying option and leave for office - I get clean and hygienically dried clothes by the time I am back at home! Machine operation is relatively quiet although after a few days it has got much louder. LG is the only company having a washing machine with built-in dryer in the market in India, so it is a monopoly as of date (2007). My major gripe is LG’s aggressive and unethical marketing strategies by which consumers can get possibly cheated - the store catalog for LG TROMM washing machines loudly advertises 7 year warranty (no conditions listed) - but after actually buying the machine I found that it has only 2 years warranty - I was told by the installation person that the rest 5 years has to be purchased separately as AMC at an additional cost of Rs 4.5K! This seems plain cheating and taking consumer for granted. It is time they were taken to consumer court and buyers made beware of their tactics! The drying time is too much, takes a minimum of 3 hours of time even for the lightest loads to get 100% drying. There is no indication on the front panel of which cycle is in progress - you need to guess from the remaining time display. The controls and indications are practically dumb.Drying is not 100%, if the full load of 3kg is used, practical drying capacity is limited to just 2kg! Anything more and I get wet clothes out of it! Its is supposed to remember where it left off when there is a power failure and resume from that point. It does this for washing, but I have not seen it do this for drying cycle. If power cuts off during drying it does not resume at all, so clothes remain wet. Their after sales service is virtually non-existent and they look for every excuse to avoid it, in my brief experience. I booked a complaint for the imperfect drying at 3kg load. I was called up by their service mechanic the next day to fix an appointment and was doing so when the call got disconnected. 3 days later when I called up the service dept I got to know that the complaint was simply "cancelled" due to "no-response"! I re-registered a complaint - this time when he called I fixed an appointment, but he never turned up. It's more than a month now since my first complaint and I am yet to see the service mechanic's face or receive any other call from them. ZERO customer service.

We replaced our old washing machine with this beast.This is a 7.2 Kg Semi Automatic Washing Machine with so many odd features to get our head wrapped around but the it gets the job done. The Wash Motor is of 360 W which is very powerful with 15 min wash timer with 25 min soak timer that rotates in couple of minutes. Spin Motor is 260 W. But the real magic is inside the wash tub.They made this new blue coloured lint collector that is so effective even dust and mud gets caught up in that mesh. Around the wash tub while washing clothes, water forces out of hundreds of nozzles into the clothes.Even below there are some rollers that act as a cloth cleaner and they all work really really well. There is one trade off though, This many features also requires a lot of water.It takes nearly 8-12 litres of water below wash tub. But still pretty decent buy.

failed_cases.message_text: Hello All, We purchased a brand-new LG washing machine fully automatic on June 3rd 2023 and this is the worst mistake we ever did. This machine was purchased from Reliance Digital. We initially were looking for other brands like Bosch and other. The executive convinced us to go with this product. Our old machine was also LG which served good for our need and expecting the same we purchased this new machine and this turned out to be nightmare to us. • From the day one we noticed the machine is damaging our clothes like cloth tearing and over rubbing compared to our old Machine. • We raised a complain about the same and the customer service received is the worst. • We had to follow up multiple times to know the status and most of time calls were left unanswered • We even shared multiple videos and images explaining our issue, still they could not find a solution for the issue. • We strongly feel there a design fault on this machine and the company is not ready to address the issue and research on the same. • Water gets clogged on the detergent filler tray and they say it is as per design and nothing can be done over the course of time if we don’t remove the water that can lead to foul smell. • The service center team wants to pickup the machine and check for the issue, we let them take the machine and with the kind of experience we are going through with the company. We asked the company people to use the original packing material before taking the machine and they refused and picked the machine with no protective cover or any kind of materials while shifting. • This is not what is expected from a brand like LG and they had the machine in their service center for a week and returned back to us like a 5 yrs old machine with dents all around the machine which is adding to wound. • Even after one week at the service center the machine is returned as it is with no changes We are forced to use our old machine even after purchasing a new one, please do not trust the brand as we did and get into a mess like this. LG does not care about customer feedback.

failed_cases.message_text: *Intro: ****LG WF**-**T7519PR** 6.5 Kg, top load **washing machine** that cares for you and your clothes. Fuzzy logic a built in load sensor automatically detects the laundry load and a microprocessor optimizes **washing** conditions such as ideal water level and **washing** time. You can set the Wash Program at Fuzzy and it would sense the appropriate **washing** method required to give your clothes the perfect wash. Turbodrum the unique technology that knows how to take care of your clothes while giving you the perfect wash. The dual direction rotating drum reduces tangling of clothes which ensures your clothes stay as fresh as and new for much longer than ever before. The **machine** also is now less susceptible to wear and tear giving you a longer life. Turbodrum allows the movement of the drum in the opposite direction of the pulsator, for a doubly-efficient wash action. I-sensor is a 7 sensor technology which automatically determines the ideal wash environment giving you the optimum wash and 100 detergent free clothes. The Sensor automatically detects detergent quantity, water temperature and water hardness so as to make best **washing** a**lg**orithm for **washing** and rinsing, ultimately giving best **washing** effect. 3 Step **Washing** makes the best **washing** performance with low tangling and is done in three steps. It gives the advantage of hand wash with best use of detergent. Air Dry when the drum rotates the air dry system sucks in air, thereby throwing a blast of air on the clothes. Thus air dry system by removing moisture leaves clothes absolutely free from bad odor & bacteria and also facilitates faster drying of clothes. Smart Clean the purpose of this cycle is to optimize the detergent residue during **washing** and rinsing cycles. In order to implement this effect, the washer adds more rinsing times with the advanced i-Sensor. The clothes are residue and detergent free. failed_cases.message_text: UrbanClap Native water purifier service is absolutely pathetic and highly misleading. The so-called 2-year warranty feels like nothing more than a marketing gimmick—it gives customers a false sense of security, but in reality, it offers almost no practical benefit. Reaching customer support itself is a frustrating experience, as you are forced to deal with a useless chatbot, and getting connected to a real person is extremely difficult. I own a Native M2 purifier, and it has been wasting an excessive amount of water. Despite clearly explaining the issue, the technician refused to take any meaningful action. He openly stated that filters and cartridges will not be replaced under any condition unless they are completely choked, even if the purifier’s performance is poor or water wastage is high. Shockingly, he didn’t even replace the pre-filter and claimed it is only changed after two years, regardless of usage or water quality, which makes absolutely no sense. This clearly shows that the service is driven by rigid policies rather than actual customer needs. You end up paying for a service plan where filters are not replaced when required, cartridges are practically never changed, and real issues like water wastage are completely ignored. The technicians simply hide behind policy excuses instead of providing solutions. Overall, this is not customer service—it’s pure negligence. Very disappointing experience and definitely not recommended.

failed_cases.message_text: Vineet ji - I know this is not a sponsored video, even though some may think otherwise. I was really waiting for this video and the testing results. I was hoping that you would continue this testing lab for a long time, which you just mentioned you will. So, I want to add a new issue to your testing and a possible solution. Issue - Low TDS dilemma. Yes, TDS is NOT the only factor to consider in water purification, however, a low TDS is equally harmful in the long run for a growing child (even adults) as heavy metals. In urban cities like your neighbour - Delhi. The municipality of Delhi supplies water with around 120-150 TDS. Putting an RO brings it to 15 (or max 30 with a mineral cartridge). I even confirmed this count with the Urban company's customer service. (Possible) Solution - Nano-filteration (NF). If you consider this in your testing and it actually works (and I hope it does), it could give a solution to two of the biggest water-related issues in India - Purification and shortage/wastage. There are a few companies that provide NF water purifiers, such as Peore, CM purifiers, and some custom-made ones as well. There may be others. Theoretically, NF purifies heavy metals and pesticides (absolute purification) and still has a high recovery rate (low wastage). So, in areas with low TDS like Delhi, NF can purify water while maintaining the optimum TDS. My proposition - If you decide to include any of the NF purifiers in your testing, I am willing to bear half of the cost of that unit, considering you test it on (my problem) the low-TDS supply. :)

analysis_excerpt.blocked_reason: High-risk symptom or smart diagnosis signal detected.

decision_after_evaluation.rule_fix_applied_in_this_step: High-risk keywords were extended with fire/flame/burnt/burn out, and high-risk decisions now force procedure_type=high_risk_troubleshooting.
next_rule_corrections: Clarification gate is too aggressive for long VOC paragraphs that already contain enough symptom context.
Remote/mode keyword matching still creates false remote_operation matches in washing machine and air purifier VOC text.
Air purifier filter replacement and filter cleaning need clearer procedure priority.
Official RAG coverage gaps for non-AC procedures cause official_evidence_required false positives.
decision_after_evaluation.llm_adapter_decision: LLM adapter should be used as non-authoritative extraction assistance for mixed Hindi/English long VOC text, especially symptom summary, product family, and clarification necessity.
decision_after_evaluation.training_classifier_decision: Do not introduce a separate training-based classifier yet. The 147-case set is currently a golden evaluation set; rule/RAG/LLM-adapter corrections should be tried before fine-tuning or local classifier training.

notes: Evaluation output is file-based because the final SQLite schema is frozen at 21 persisted tables.
expected_intent means expected service_flow_type: self_care, self_as, or expert_as.
no_match_precision treats official_match_review_needed/official_evidence_required as predicted no-match actions.
LLM is not the final authority; rules, RAG evidence, and DecisionEngineV2 remain authoritative.
